blocks.content: Tools should do one thing and return structured data. Avoid returning raw HTML, giant nested blobs, or unformatted text logs to the LLM, as this wastes tokens.

items.title: Single Responsibility
items.content: One function should solve one exact query (e.g., look up order vs edit order).

items.title: Filtering Blobs
items.content: Discard metadata fields (like headers, tracing keys) before returning data to the agent.

items.title: Error Boundaries
items.content: Never throw uncaught generic exceptions; catch them and return error messages.

blocks.content:
Question 1: Why should tool returns be compact and structured?
- A) It compiles the project faster.
- B) To minimize token consumption and guide clean LLM generation.
- C) It is required by the OAuth standard.

Feedback: Option B is correct. Keeping responses minimal saves token costs.
